LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KARINA GARCIA'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED IN LARGE PART. MS. GARCIA IS AWARDED ATTORNEY'S FEES OF $80,000 AND COSTS OF $15,692.59 WITHOUT A MULTIPLIER. AFTER REVIEW OF THE PAPERS FILED ON THIS MOTION AND THE DOCKET, THE COURT BELIEVES THAT THE AMOUNT OF ATTORNEY HOURS IN MS. GARCIA'S LODESTAR CALCULATION ARE EXCESSIVE GIVEN THE NATURE OF THE CASE AND THE NECESSARY WORK FOR THIS CASE AND THUS REDUCES THE FEES SOUGHT FROM $93,096.50 TO $80,000. THE COURT FINDS THAT ALL COSTS CLAIMED BY MS. GARCIA WERE REASONABLY INCURRED AND THE HOURLY RATES OF HER COUNSEL ARE ALSO REASONABLE. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR A MULTIPLIER IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS NOTHING EXTRAORDINARY ABOUT EITHER THE RESULT ACHIEVED OR THE WORK NECESSARY TO ACHIEVE THAT RESULT AND THE AWARD OF $80,000 IN FEES SUFFICIENTLY COMPENSATES MS. GARCIA'S COUNSEL FOR THE WORK THEY DID ON THE THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Continued To Jan-31-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs, And Expenses
Off Calendar Dec-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Karina Garcia's motion for attorney's fees and costs is granted in large part. Ms. Garcia is awarded attorney's fees of $80,000 and costs of $15,692.59 without a multiplier. After review of the papers filed on this motion and the docket, the court believes that the amount of attorney hours in Ms. Garcia's lodestar calculation are excessive given the nature of the case and the necessary work for this case and thus reduces the fees sought from $93,096.50 to $80,000. The court finds that all costs claimed by Ms. Garcia were reasonably incurred and the hourly rates of her counsel are also reasonable. Plaintiffs' request for a multiplier is denied because there is nothing extraordinary about either the result achieved or the work necessary to achieve that result and the award of $80,000 in fees sufficiently compensates Ms. Garcia's counsel for the work they did on the this case. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
